skill.name: sentry-automation
skill.description: Automate Sentry tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): manage issues/events, configure alerts, track releases, monitor projects and teams. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Sentry error tracking and monitoring tasks through Claude using Composio Rube MCP integration.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill provides legitimate automation for Sentry error tracking through Composio Rube MCP. The static findings flagged as external_commands are false positives - they detect MCP tool names in documentation (e.g., SENTRY_LIST_AN_ORGANIZATIONS_ISSUES), not shell execution. The network reference is to a documented MCP server endpoint (rube.app/mcp) that users explicitly configure. No actual security risks identified.

low_findings.title: MCP Tool Name References Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The static analyzer detected 88 instances of backtick-wrapped text flagged as 'external_commands'. These are MCP tool names (e.g., SENTRY_LIST_AN_ORGANIZATIONS_ISSUES, RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS) documented in the skill, not actual shell command execution. This is a false positive - the skill only calls Sentry API tools through the MCP integration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All 88 occurrences are MCP tool name references in documentation, not runtime command execution. The skill only uses the Rube MCP interface to call Sentry APIs.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Reference
low_findings.description: Line 22 contains a URL reference to rube.app/mcp. This is a documented MCP server endpoint that users must explicitly configure in their AI client. This is legitimate, user-initiated configuration, not hidden network activity.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is explicitly documented in setup instructions and requires user action to configure. No automatic network requests are made by the skill.

content.value_statement: This skill enables Claude to manage Sentry error tracking operations including issue investigation, alert configuration, release tracking, and cron monitoring through Composio Rube MCP integration.
actual_capabilities: List and investigate Sentry issues across organizations and projects
Create and manage alert rules for projects and organizations
Track software releases and deployments with source maps
Monitor and update cron job monitoring configurations
Query issues using Sentry search syntax (is:unresolved, assigned:me)
List and manage organization teams and member visibility
limitations: Does not fix code bugs directly - only manages monitoring configuration
Does not access raw application database or runtime data
Does not manage billing, user permissions, or team access settings
Requires active Rube MCP connection to Sentry for functionality

use_cases.title: Production Error Investigation
use_cases.description: Developer uses Claude to quickly find and investigate critical errors in production by querying Sentry issues with specific filters.

use_cases.title: Alert Rule Configuration
use_cases.description: DevOps engineer creates and updates alert rules for new projects or modified thresholds using natural language commands.

use_cases.title: Release Monitoring
use_cases.description: SRE monitors new releases and their associated errors by tracking deployment events and issue correlations.

prompt_templates.title: List Recent Errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the sentry-automation skill to list the 10 most recent unresolved errors in my production project. Show the issue title, frequency, and last seen timestamp.

prompt_templates.title: Create Alert Rule
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new alert rule for my backend-api project that triggers when error rate exceeds 5% over 10 minutes. Notify the on-call team.

prompt_templates.title: Track New Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new release version 2.1.0 for my organization, mark it as deployed to production environment, and upload source maps from the dist folder.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Project Triage
prompt_templates.prompt: Search all projects in my organization for issues tagged with 'database' that have occurred more than 50 times in the last 24 hours. Group them by project and suggest priority.

output_examples.input: List unresolved errors in my backend-api project
output_examples.output:
Found 23 unresolved issues in backend-api:

🔴 Critical (3):
- Connection timeout in Redis client (seen 234 times)
- Memory leak in worker process (seen 89 times)
- Uncaught promise rejection (seen 67 times)

🟡 Warning (8):
- Deprecated API endpoint called (seen 45 times)
- Slow database query > 2s (seen 32 times)
...

Would you like me to investigate any specific issue?

output_examples.input: Create an alert for high error rate
output_examples.output:
Alert rule created successfully:

Name: High Error Rate - backend-api
Condition: Error rate > 5% in 10 minutes
Actions: Email to on-call team, Slack #alerts
Project: backend-api
Status: Active

The alert will start monitoring immediately.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to verify current tool schemas before workflow execution
Verify Rube MCP connection status shows ACTIVE before running any Sentry operations
Use specific query filters (is:unresolved, assigned:me, times-seen:>10) to reduce API calls and improve response time
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode organization or project names - always use lookup tools to get current slugs
Do not skip connection status verification - operations will fail if MCP connection is not active
Do not ignore rate limit responses - implement backoff to avoid throttling

faq.question: Does this skill work with self-hosted Sentry?
faq.answer: Yes, if your self-hosted Sentry instance is connected via the Rube MCP sentry toolkit. The skill works with any Sentry API-compatible endpoint.

faq.question: What authentication is needed?
faq.answer: OAuth authentication is handled through Rube when you connect your Sentry account. The skill uses the permissions granted during the OAuth flow.

faq.question: Can I manage multiple Sentry organizations?
faq.answer: Yes, specify the organization slug in each request. Use SENTRY_GET_ORGANIZATION_DETAILS to list accessible organizations.

faq.question: Can this skill create new Sentry issues?
faq.answer: No, this skill only reads and manages existing issues. To create issues from your application, configure Sentry SDK in your code.

faq.question: What notification channels are supported for alerts?
faq.answer: Alert actions depend on your Sentry organization configuration. Common channels include Slack, email, PagerDuty, and webhooks.

faq.question: How do I fix connection not active errors?
faq.answer: Run RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit sentry. If status is not ACTIVE, follow the returned auth link to complete OAuth, then verify again.
